label: The mirror
sub: what I see when it’s just me
framing: Where I argue with myself. The quadrant I write in when I don’t know what I believe yet — where first drafts go to be held up to the light.

items.title: UI needs to serve a goal
items.dek: Good visuals aren't about perfection. They're about clarity.

items.title: Making AI feel human
items.dek: Humanity, in interfaces, is not about imitation.

items.title: What I’m good at. What I’m not.
items.dek: And who I want to work with.

label: The practice
framing: What I kick off when nobody asked. Meetups, experiments, rituals I run on myself — the stuff that wouldn’t exist if I waited for permission.

items.title: Vibe-coding meetup at Apple Park
items.dek: An informal lunch for people curious about the new tools.

items.title: Friends of friends at work
items.dek: I want my friends to be friends with each other.

items.title: Making design fun
items.dek: Caltrain, After Effects, and a stubborn suspicion that everything could be easier.

items.title: Meet the stranger challenge
items.dek: Twenty minutes with someone I’ve never met — this is what made me start paying attention →

label: Paying attention
sub: paying attention to what I notice in other people
framing: Watching, listening, not-yet-acting. Most of what I’ve learned about design I’ve learned by watching people describe their work out loud.

label: The work
framing: The finished, shipped, collaborative stuff. Where thinking has to become a decision and other people have to live with it.

items.title: Collaboration: the good side and the annoying side
All the things we’ve shipped together
